title: 0.5 M EDTA (0.5 L) Preparation
author: Mattia Pegoraro
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xr2fm8e

steps.title: Prepare beaker with water
steps.instruction: Fill 0.5 L beker with 300 mL of deionized water.

steps.title: Add EDTA Disodium Salt
steps.instruction: Add 73.06 g of Ethylenediamine Tetraacetic Acid Disodium Salt (292,24 g/mol) to the water and mix.

steps.title: Adjust pH to 8.0
steps.instruction: Add NaOH pellets until the pH is near 7.8 (the solution will be clear around pH 7.5). Complete to arrive at pH 8 with NaOH 5 M solution (also NaOH 10 M is good).

steps.title: Adjust volume to 500 mL
steps.instruction: Once at pH 8, fill to 500 mL .

steps.title: Sterilize EDTA solution
steps.instruction: Autoclave at 121 °C for 00:15:00 or filter it with 0,22 u filter. .

steps.title: Verify final pH
steps.instruction: Put the electrode pH meter into the solution (with continuos mixing) and mesure the pH (don't worry if the solution isn't clear). The pH will be about 3 at this point. The pH needs to be at 8.
